auth.dontHaveWallet: Don't have a wallet? Create one
auth.enterPasswordToContinue: Please enter your wallet password to continue.
auth.enterValidSeed: Please enter a valid seed phrase to continue.
auth.forkDetected: A fork has been detected in your transaction history! This usually happens if you restore an old backup or use this wallet on multiple devices simultaneously without proper synchronization.
auth.forkRecoveryRequired: To ensure your identity and reputation are protected, you must perform a full wallet recovery using your seed phrase.
auth.functionsSuspended: All normal wallet functions are suspended until recovery is complete.
auth.helpHandoverLong: To guarantee the highest security for your balance, your wallet can only execute transactions actively on one device at a time. When switching to another device, you must perform a 'handover'. The previous device is then paused for new payments until you return the rights there as needed.
auth.helpHandoverShort: Transfers active access rights to this device.
auth.helpProfileLong: A profile stores your vouchers, contacts, and transaction history securely encrypted on this exact device. You can create multiple profiles to manage different digital identities separately.
auth.helpProfileShort: Your local, encrypted vault for this device.
auth.loadExistingWalletSub: (Creates a wallet from an existing seed)
auth.noProfilesToRecover: No local profiles detected for restoration.
auth.passphrasePlaceholder: Enter extra word if you used one during creation
auth.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
auth.seedPhrasePlaceholder: Enter your 12 or 24 word seed phrase here
auth.selectProfileToRecover: Please select a profile to recover
common.bugReportDescription: Inspect your local application log file, copy it to the clipboard, and report issues on GitHub.
common.bugReportWarnDesc: Logs do not contain passwords or private keys, but they might contain file paths or usernames. Please review them before copying.
common.prefixInfoA1: When you use the same seed phrase on multiple devices, they all share the same base cryptographic key (did:key:...). To prevent the devices from clashing (e.g. using the same transaction sequence numbers), each device needs a unique prefix (like laptop or phone). This splits the wallet into separate, safe branches.
common.prefixInfoA2: Yes! The prefix is visible to your contacts (e.g., my-company:abc@did:key:...). Using a recognizable name (like your name or business name) makes it extremely easy for your partners to quickly identify and verify payments from you.
common.prefixInfoA3: Yes, it is optional. If you only use this wallet on this single device, leave the field empty. This creates a standard "Root Account". You do not lose any features or security.
common.prefixInfoA4: A prefix is permanently tied to a profile once it is created. However, you can always create another profile (sub-account) on the same device using the same seed phrase but with a different prefix. You do not need to delete your old profile to do this.
common.prefixInfoModalSubtitle: Learn how to configure your multi-device sub-accounts safely.
common.prefixInfoQ1: ❓ What is a Device Prefix / Sub-Account?
common.prefixInfoQ2: 🏢 Can I use it for branding / recognition?
common.prefixInfoQ3: ⚙️ Is it optional? What if I leave it blank?
common.prefixInfoQ4: ✏️ Can I change or set the prefix later?
common.prefixInfoSafety1: Every active device using the same seed phrase must use a different prefix.
common.prefixInfoSafety2: Using the exact same prefix (including leaving it blank) on two devices at the same time will cause sequence collisions. This will result in severe errors and inevitably damage your reputation in the network.
common.supportContributeDesc: Bring in your skills as a developer, designer, or organizer.
common.supportDescription: This app is supported by the association 'Menschlich Miteinander'. We promote the development of free tools for a decentralized and self-determined exchange. If you share our vision, you can support the project through a donation, membership, or active participation. You can find all details on our website.
common.supportDonateDesc: Support the development financially through a direct contribution.
common.supportMemberDesc: Become part of our community and actively shape the future.
conflict.evidenceDescription: Detailed transactions demonstrating the double-spend. The earliest transaction is considered valid.
conflict.fetchDetailsFailed: Failed to fetch double-spend proof details: {{error}}
conflict.noConflictsDescription: Your wallet is currently healthy. No conflicting transactions have been detected.
conflict.offenderDescription: This identity has double-spent assets. All transactions signed by this offender are marked untrusted.
conflict.protocolRulesDescription: Transactions must be strictly sequenced. Once a state is spent, any subsequent transaction referencing that same state is a double-spend. Offending vouchers are quarantined to maintain system integrity.
conflict.resolutionDescription: If you have resolved this dispute offline with the counterparty (e.g., they gave you cash or standard currency to compensate for the blocked voucher), you can override this conflict locally to unblock your wallet.
conflict.resolutionPlaceholder: Describe how this dispute was settled...
conflict.title: Fraud Reports & Conflicts
conflict.verifySecurityDescription: Please enter your wallet password to authorize this local conflict override.
conflict.victimDescription: A double-spend attempt was detected! The offender issued two conflicting transactions referencing the same voucher state. This is a violation of the network protocol. Your local balance has been quarantined.
conflict.witnessDescription: This double-spend was observed in the network, but does not directly affect your balance. It is shown here for transparency and security monitoring.
contacts.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to remove this contact from your address book?
contacts.description: Manage your verified identities and local address book.
contacts.emptyDescription: No contacts match your current filter. Add your first contact to get started.
contacts.title: Contacts
dashboard.conceptDetailedText: We are used to using a global financial system that promises security and convenience. A look under the hood shows: when we take out a loan today, the bank creates new money from our promise to repay it. This created money then serves the general public as a means of payment. The principle of money creation from a trustworthy promise works exceptionally well. What if we transferred this principle to our direct, local level? This app enables exactly this paradigm shift. It is a tool with which you can digitally secure your own promises of performance (vouchers) and use them as currency. You perform a service or guarantee it, issue your voucher for it, and the recipient can simply pass it on digitally to third parties. This is how you make your own value creation liquid.
dashboard.conceptDetailedTitle: From Promise of Payment to Digital Currency
dashboard.conceptFutureText: This system is currently in the testing phase. What you see here is the absolute foundation – the basis of a completely new paradigm. But the architecture is deliberately kept extremely flexible and opens the door to far-reaching possibilities. From the perspective of the individual user or an institution, this concept already offers enormous sovereignty and independence to actively shape economic coexistence. If the ecosystem continues to grow, potentially every person, every community, every initiative, or every economic circle is able to put their own decentralized currency into circulation or establish customized clearing systems. Especially in times of crisis, this enables the local economy to react extremely flexibly and keep vital economic cycles intact. This robustness is also shown in a very practical way: even in the event of major power outages, the offline capability ensures that payments run smoothly via battery-powered mobile devices. Possible fraud or conflicts can then simply be quickly detected and resolved during subsequent online reconciliation. The principle scales seamlessly: creation is designed for the individual person, but theoretically, states could also issue sovereign currencies on the same basis. In theory, the concept offers unprecedented data protection, since the essential transaction information lies directly in the files that are exchanged in encrypted form. At the same time, the system is designed in such a way that it can be extended in the future to include an anonymous online check in the form of a decentralized blocklist register. This additional support from servers then theoretically allows real-time payments, as we are used to today, as fraud by double-spending is immediately and preventively excluded. Once this point is reached, the required trust for a payment is reduced solely to the issuer of the value claim. If this issuer is then adequately secured by guarantors, insurance, municipalities, or states, a full-fledged, highly scalable payment system arises. One that is in no way inferior to today's in convenience – but is fundamentally more robust due to its decentralized character and never deprives people of their ability to act.
dashboard.conceptFutureTitle: The Launch and Outlook: A Proof of Concept with Enormous Potential
dashboard.conceptIdentityText: Classical cryptocurrencies usually rely on anonymity. This system takes the opposite path: the path of human trust. In this app, you do not act via disposable addresses, but via a fixed, cryptographic identity (a so-called did:key). This identity is linked to you as an actor. Anyone who acts in the network and keeps their promises builds a measurable reputation over time – the most valuable asset in a decentralized economy. To give you full organizational freedom, you can create any number of 'sub-accounts' under your main identity. This allows you to neatly separate payments for your local craft, private neighborhood help, or voluntary projects, and thus use the core reputation built up in different contexts.
dashboard.conceptIdentityTitle: Identity Instead of Anonymity: Your Reputation Counts
dashboard.conceptPrivacyText: One of the most important features of this system is real privacy. There is no central server and no global blockchain that permanently stores every one of your expenses in a public ledger. Each voucher is an isolated, self-contained file – a so-called container. This container carries its complete, cryptographically secured transaction history within itself. When you make a payment, you only transfer this file directly (peer-to-peer) to your counterpart. Only those who receive this container directly have insight into the transactions. Since no global network consensus is required, this even works offline. No one outside the transaction chain learns about this exchange.
dashboard.conceptScreen1Text: In today's financial system, new money is created when loans are issued – so it is based on a promise of payment. This app uses exactly this proven principle, but puts it in your hands: you can create your own digital vouchers backed by your skills, your time, or tangible assets. Create promises of performance and use them as digital currency in your network.
dashboard.conceptScreen2Text: In this system, you are not an anonymous account number. Your wallet uses a fixed, cryptographic identity (a so-called did:key). This means: every one of your vouchers is permanently linked to you. With every promise you redeem, you build real reputation in the network. For a better overview, you can flexibly create different sub-accounts under your main identity – for example, for private favors, your business, or special projects.
dashboard.conceptScreen3Text: A voucher is only worth as much as the trust in its issuer. To give your vouchers more weight, you can add acquaintances: 'witnesses' merely confirm that they know you and that you are a real person. 'Guarantors' go a step further and are liable for your promise if you are ever unable to redeem it. This creates a decentralized, resilient network of trust.
dashboard.conceptScreen4Text: Unlike many cryptocurrencies, there is no global blockchain that publicly stores all transactions forever. Your vouchers are small, self-contained files (containers). When you pay, you transfer this file directly to your counterpart – this even works offline! Your transactions remain a purely private matter between the involved parties.
dashboard.conceptScreen5Text: This app is a 'Proof of Concept' for a completely new paradigm. Since the system is highly private and operates offline, there is no central server to pre-verify and block transactions. Errors or intentional fraud (double-spending) are only detected after a delay due to the mathematics. The golden rule: take responsibility for your actions. Initially, only use the system in your trusted local environment or try out the mechanics completely risk-free with test vouchers.
dashboard.conceptSecurityText: Where there is great freedom, there is also personal responsibility. Since transactions take place offline and completely privately, there is (currently) no global guard to check at the same moment whether you spend a value twice, accidentally or intentionally (double-spending). However, the system is by no means defenseless. It uses advanced mathematics: every fraud attempt forces the perpetrator on a cryptographic level to trigger an 'identity trap'. The fraud leaves forge-proof evidence in the file that clearly reveals the identity of the perpetrator. The inevitable loss of reputation is far greater damage in this system than a short-term gain.
dashboard.conceptSecurityTitle: Security: Fraud Detection Instead of Preventive Blocking
dashboard.conceptSubtitle: Learn how this app works, from voucher creation to privacy.
dashboard.conceptTrustText: A self-written voucher works wonderfully in the close neighborhood. But how is it accepted supra-regionally? The answer is: through social confirmation and clear standards. You can ask other people to digitally co-sign your voucher. A witness merely confirms your identity ('I know this person'). A guarantor, on the other hand, creates maximum commitment by guaranteeing: 'If this person does not deliver, I will stand in for the value.' In addition, the system offers unlimited flexibility through standards. A voucher does not necessarily have to mean '1 hour of work'. The true strength of the system lies in the fact that these standards are defined by the community itself. A standard defines the rules of the game – for example, what the value refers to and who must sign it for the voucher to be accepted as valid in the network. Currently, two test standards are integrated in the app for testing: FreeTaler: A simple standard with no real equivalent value. It serves primarily as an example to test the purely technical mechanics of sending 'Talers' safely. For simplicity, a FreeTaler does not require any additional signatures and can be used immediately. Minuto: This standard maps the concrete concept of human money (backed by qualitative life time). For this standard, the rules require the additional signature of guarantors for the voucher to become valid. Here, the concept of social commitment can be put to the test in practice. In the future, communities and institutions can define their own standards. Vouchers can be linked to precious metals, to existing currencies like the Euro, or to specific local goods. Institutions can act as trust anchors and certify certain standards. You decide which rules you submit to.
dashboard.conceptTrustTitle: How Trust Scales: Witnesses, Guarantors, and Standards
dashboard.profileIncompleteDesc: Building trust requires a complete identity.
dashboard.securityWarningDesc: Action required to repair your wallet.
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.welcomeSubtitle: Manage your community vouchers and local currency.

integrity.integrityOutdatedDescription: The security integrity record does not match the current wallet state epoch. Possible rollback attempt.
integrity.invalidSignatureDescription: The security integrity record signature is invalid. The record itself might have been tampered with.
integrity.manipulatedItemsDescription: Data items have been modified outside of this application. This is a security risk.
integrity.missingIntegrityRecordDescript: Your wallet does not have a security integrity record yet. You should create one to enable protection.
integrity.missingItemsDescription: Some critical wallet data items are missing from the disk. This could lead to data loss.
integrity.repairInstruction: By repairing the wallet, you accept the current state of items as valid and sign a new security integrity record.
integrity.unknownItemsDescription: Unrecognized data items were found in your wallet directory.
profile.autoAddressTooltip: Resolve coordinates from the address above
profile.autoCleaningHint: Automatic cleaning handles numbers (e.g. 1. 2.), dots, newlines and extra spaces.
profile.creatingProgress: Creating profile, please wait... This may take a moment.
profile.criticalPrefix: Critical: Every active device MUST have a unique prefix to prevent irreversible reputation loss.
profile.dataEncrypted: Your data is securely encrypted on this device.
profile.devicePrefixExplanation: This abbreviation cleanly separates the balances on your devices from one another. If you enter an abbreviation that you already use on another device, that account will be loaded here. If you choose a new abbreviation, a fresh sub-account for this device is created.
profile.doubleSpendWarning: This wallet is now bound to this instance. DO NOT use this profile on the old device. Simultaneous use will cause permanent reputation damage and asset locking.
profile.helpMnemonicLong: These 12 or 24 words form the irrevocable foundation of your account. Since the system is completely decentralized, there is no support team that can reset your password. If you lose these words, your digital identity and all your vouchers are lost forever. Keep the words offline and absolutely secure!
profile.helpMnemonicShort: The master key to your digital identity and wallet.
profile.helpPassphraseLong: The passphrase serves as extra security. Even if someone steals your seed phrase, your wallet remains inaccessible without this passphrase. IMPORTANT: If you forget this passphrase, you cannot recover your wallet - not even with the correct seed phrase!
profile.helpPassphraseShort: Additional protection (like a 25th word) for your seed phrase.
profile.helpSubaccountLong: If you want to use your identity on multiple devices (e.g. mobile and PC), each device needs its own identifier. This option adds a visible prefix to your public identity. This is particularly interesting for companies to prefix the company name, or privately to mark your own name or the respective device (e.g. 'Mobile'). This way, the system always knows which device an action comes from, and all your devices work together smoothly.
profile.helpSubaccountShort: Secure use on multiple devices using a name identifier.
profile.localPasswordLabel: Set local password for this device
profile.loginSelectProfile: Please select a profile and enter your password.
profile.needsPlaceholder: Describe what you are looking for...
profile.passphraseAdvanced: Advanced: This word is part of your backup. You MUST save it along with the other {{wordCount}} words!
profile.passphraseIfUsedPlaceholder: Enter extra security word if used
profile.passphraseMismatch: Error: The passphrases do not match
profile.passwordMinLength: Error: Password must be at least 8 characters
profile.passwordMismatch: Error: The passwords do not match
profile.pasteSeedPhrasePlaceholder: Paste full seed phrase here... Numbers and punctuation are cleaned automatically.
profile.prefixDescription: Required if using the same seed phrase on multiple devices. Can also be used as a public identifier (e.g. company name) for easy recognition. You can create multiple profiles on this device in parallel without deleting old ones.
profile.recoverStep1Subtitle: Enter your existing 12 or 24-word secret seed phrase.
profile.recoverStep2Subtitle: Create a name and password for this new profile.
profile.recoveryRequiredDescription: Your security seal has been lost. You must perform a recovery to continue using this wallet.
profile.restoreWarning: If you haven't moved this wallet (Handover), EVERYTHING WILL BE LOST PERMANENTLY. Only proceed if you have a verified backup on another device or your master key sequence.
profile.seedMismatch: Error: The phrase does not match
profile.seedWarning: Never share this phrase with anyone. Digital copies are highly discouraged.
profile.seedWordsIncorrect: Error: One or more words are incorrect
profile.serviceOfferPlaceholder: Describe what you bring to the network...
profile.step1Subtitle: This unique word sequence is your physical backup. Store it offline.
profile.step2Subtitle: Confirm the key sequence to ensure backup integrity.
profile.step3Subtitle: Create your access credentials for this device.
profile.subAccountDescription: Check this if you plan to use this seed phrase on more than one device (e.g. computer and phone) at the same time.
profile.subAccountOption: Set up as sub-account / for multiple devices (Optional)
profile.walletSecurityDesc: Your identity is stored encrypted on your local device. It is only shared with counterparties when you explicitly sign a voucher.
settings.deviceBindingDescription: This unique identifier binds your wallet cryptographic seals to this physical device installation to prevent unauthorized cloning.

settings.languageDescription: Choose the language for the user interface.
settings.privacyProtocolsDescription: How visible are you when sending vouchers?
settings.systemPreferencesDescription: Manage your identity, security protocols and network defaults.
settings.title: Settings
transfer.autoSelection: Automatic selection will prioritize optimal ledger fragmentation.
transfer.helpPrivacyLong:
**Stealth Mode:** Anonymizes your signature in the transaction chain. Prevents surveillance by potential spies in the transaction chain. Identity is only disclosed if a double-spend is detected.

**Public Mode:** Standard transparency for maximum visibility. Builds trust in closed networks and local community circles.
transfer.helpPrivacyShort: Choose between Stealth (private) and Public (transparent) transaction execution.
transfer.helpSenderNameLong: When enabled, the direct recipient of this transfer will see this name in addition to your cryptographic address (did:key) – similar to the account holder name in a bank transfer. For subsequent recipients down the chain (e.g. in stealth mode), your identity remains hidden.
transfer.helpSenderNameShort: Shows the recipient who the payment is from.
transfer.importFile.acceptResponsibility: I accept responsibility for potential double-spend conflicts.
transfer.importFile.dropZoneHint: Support for .transfer, .ask, and .sig files received from trusted parties.
transfer.importFile.duplicateSignatureDe: This signature is already present on the selected asset.
transfer.importFile.formatSigReqDesc: Sign an asset for a peer.
transfer.importFile.localProcessing: ALL PROCESSING IS PERFORMED LOCALLY ON THIS DEVICE
transfer.importFile.passwordHint: Required only if the sender encrypted this specific payload with a password.
transfer.importFile.title: Import File
transfer.importFile.transferCompleteDesc: The transfer has been successfully validated and added to your wallet.
transfer.privacyConflict: Privacy Conflict: Mixed privacy requirements in selection.
transfer.publicModeDesc: Standard transparency. Builds high trust in closed networks and community circles.
transfer.receiveSuccess.conflictDesc: Our security system has detected that someone tried to spend the same money twice. One of the vouchers you just received is affected and has been safely 'quarantined' for your protection.
transfer.receiveSuccess.conflictTitle: Security Warning: Problem with your Balance
transfer.receiveSuccess.normalSuccessDes: Assets have been added to your wallet.
transfer.receiveSuccess.securityIssueAff: A security issue affects your new assets.
transfer.receiveSuccess.witnessDesc: Your own balance is completely safe and unaffected. However, while processing this transfer, your wallet discovered a fraud attempt happening elsewhere in the network. This information helps keep the community safe.
transfer.receiveSuccess.witnessOnlyDesc: Assets added. Your wallet also helped the network.
transfer.reputationAlertDesc: This identity has a flagged trust status. Exercise caution.
transfer.stealthModeDesc: Anonymizes your signature in the transaction chain. Prevents surveillance by potential spies in the transaction chain. Reveal occurs only upon double-spend detection.
transfer.success.description: Your funds are securely packaged and ready for delivery.
transfer.success.nextStepDesc: Save the transfer file and send it to your contact (e.g. via Signal, WhatsApp, or Email).
transfer.success.title: Transfer Ready to Send
transfer.title: Send Vouchers
voucher.create.confirmDescription: This action will cryptographically sign the asset with your private key. The voucher will be permanently attributed to your identity. Proceed?
voucher.create.nextStepSign: You will sign this voucher in the next step.
voucher.create.publicDataNoticeDesc: The information embedded in this voucher serves as your cryptographic guarantee. All creator details will be permanently readable by anyone verifying this asset on the public ledger.
voucher.create.testWarningDesc: You are about to issue a real redeemable voucher. In this early development phase, we recommend using Test Assets to prevent potential asset loss.
voucher.export.title: Export Signature Request
voucher.helpCreatorLong: The creator is the person or institution who originally issued this voucher and guarantees its redemption. The ID (DID) is the unique digital address of the creator.
voucher.helpCreatorShort: The creator is the person or institution who originally issued this voucher.
voucher.helpModeLong: Test vouchers have no real value and are only used to try out app mechanics in a sandbox. Real vouchers represent a commitment to deliver real goods or services and are meant for live usage.
voucher.helpModeShort: Test vouchers are for trying features; real vouchers represent a commitment.
voucher.helpSignRequestLong: A voucher requires additional signatures from trusted co-signers (guarantors) to be accepted as a valid means of payment in the network. You can export a signing request and send it to your contacts.
voucher.helpSignRequestShort: Co-signatures (guarantors) are required to make this voucher valid.
voucher.helpSignaturesLong: A witness merely confirms the identity of the issuer ('I know this person'). A guarantor also guarantees the value of the voucher ('If the issuer does not deliver, I guarantee the value').
voucher.helpSignaturesShort: Signatures confirm the issuer's identity (witness) or guarantee the value (guarantor).
voucher.mode.realDescription: Creates a real voucher. This represents a promise to deliver real goods or services.
voucher.mode.testDescription: Test vouchers have no real value and only serve to try out the app mechanics. They cannot be redeemed for real goods or services.
voucher.removeSignatureConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this signature? This will be recorded and might affect the voucher's validity.
voucher.role.creator: Creator
voucher.rule.genderBalanceFemale: According to ISO 5218, exactly one female (2) guarantor is required.
voucher.rule.genderBalanceMale: According to ISO 5218, exactly one male (1) guarantor is required.
voucher.signRequest.description: Review and cryptographically sign the requested asset endorsement.
voucher.signRequest.embedProfileData: Embed your public profile data in the signature
voucher.signRequest.impactUnavailable: Impact data unavailable for this role configuration.
voucher.signRequest.localSignatureNotice: SIGNATURE IS GENERATED LOCALLY AND NEVER EXPOSES YOUR PRIVATE KEY
voucher.signRequest.openRuleCheck: Note: An open rule checks for your {{field}}
voucher.signRequest.selectRoleForImpact: Please select a role to evaluate the endorsement impact.
voucher.signRequest.signatureValid: This signature is valid and ready for use.
voucher.signRequest.standardViolation: Standard Violation: The specified role is not authorized for this asset.
voucher.signRequest.title: Signature Request
